title: LogLineReceipt
description: Nine-slot accountable act. Three hash layers: tuple (pure 9 slots), content (slots + aux), envelope (slots + aux + transport).

receipt_version.description: Frozen at v0. Implementations that emit any other value are not conformant.

when.description: ISO-8601 date-time when authoritative; free string otherwise. The slot is authoritative; format is recommended but not enforced at v0.

json_canonicalization.description: Canonicalization profile used for all three hashes. Frozen at jcs-rfc8785 in v0.

result.description: FORBIDDEN. Earlier drafts placed result/evidence inside the receipt; v0 keeps the receipt narrow. Result is an upstream concern, not a receipt field.

evidence.description: FORBIDDEN. Same rationale as result. Engines that need evidence should attach it via a separate evidence record content-addressed by this receipt's id.

transport.description: FORBIDDEN at the receipt level. Transport metadata belongs ONLY to the Envelope wrapper.

x-logline-aux-fields.description: Any top-level field not in the required nine slots / hashes / version / canonicalization is AUX. AUX is free-form and included in content_hash. AUX is NOT included in tuple_hash. AUX MUST NOT shadow reserved field names. AUX MUST NOT use any of the forbidden-legacy names.

tuple_hash.description: Identifies the pure act (nine slots, nothing else). Stable across re-interpretations.

content_hash.description: Identifies the interpreted act (slots + aux + meta, but NOT transport). The receipt id equals this value.

envelope_hash.description: Identifies the transported package. Lives ONLY on the Envelope wrapper. The receipt itself has no envelope_hash field. Computed by the sender at every boundary crossing; verified by the receiver before accepting content.

Hashes.description: Hashes computed at receipt emission. envelope_hash deliberately does NOT live here — it is a property of the transport wrapper (Envelope), not of the resting receipt.

Envelope.description: Transport wrapper. Computed by sender. content is the full receipt (with its own emission-time hashes); envelope_hash covers {content, transport} canonicalized together.
